fullResponse.name: Binary Search Visualization
fullResponse.chatResponse: I will show you how Binary Search efficiently finds a value in a sorted array by repeatedly halving the search space.

steps.title: Search Space Initialization
steps.content: Binary Search requires a **sorted array**. We initialize two pointers: `low` (index 0) and `high` (the last index). The algorithm only considers elements within this inclusive range.

steps.title: Finding the Midpoint
steps.content: In each step, we calculate the middle index: `mid = low + floor((high - low) / 2)`. We then compare `array[mid]` with our `target` value.

steps.title: Eliminating Half
steps.content: If the `target` is smaller than the middle element, it must be in the left half, so we update `high = mid - 1`. If it's larger, it must be in the right half, so we update `low = mid + 1`. This gives Binary Search its $O(\log n)$ time complexity.
